findings.summary: LDO output voltage estimated at 1.58V for TPS76601 instead of ~3.19V; BAT-MIDPOINT net classified as 'interrupt' in net_classification; Assembly complexity reports 0 THT components, but PCB shows 2...

correct.description: The BQ34Z100 uses HDQ/I2C. The analyzer correctly found the I2C bus with proper pull-up resistors R35 and R36 on SDA and SCL respectively.

Component classification is accurate: BQ24103 detected as 'battery management', correct subcircuit grouping, feedback divider (R25/R26) for LDO identified. Core component detection is working well despite missing MPNs.

The rescue library uses 'unspecified' pin types throughout, so the analyzer cannot determine that U5 TPS76601 provides a power_out on +3V3. ERC would indeed flag these nets in KiCad. The warnings accurately reflect what KiCad ERC would report.

incorrect.description: TPS76601 has Vref=1.21V but analyzer used 0.6V heuristic. With R25=270k/R26=165k feedback divider: correct Vout = 1.21*(1+270k/165k) = 3.19V. Analyzer estimates 0.6*(1+270k/165k) = 1.58V, which is wrong by 2x. The power_budget also reports output_voltage=1.582V for this rail.

The net named BAT-MIDPOINT (midpoint voltage tap between two Li-ion cells for the BQ34Z100 fuel gauge) is classified as 'interrupt'. The name heuristic appears to match '-MIDPOINT' suffix incorrectly. It is a voltage sensing signal, not an interrupt.

The schematic assembly_complexity says smd_count=100, tht_count=0. The PCB analyzer correctly reports tht_count=28. The schematic analyzer cannot detect THT from custom Mlab library footprint names (Straight_1x02, Straight_2x04, WAGO256, Drzak_2410) since it lacks a footprint keyword lookup for non-standard libraries.

design_analysis.power_domains lists U3 (BQ34Z100), U4 (SN74HC164D), and U5 (TPS76601) all on +3V3, but power_budget only includes U4 with 10mA estimate. U3 and U5 are omitted from the load calculation. ic_count=1 is wrong; should be at least 2.

findings.summary: Board dimensions (80.77x60.45mm), layer count (2), component counts (100 total, 28 THT, 72 SMD) all correct

correct.description: Statistics are consistent with the gerber output dimensions and layer set. Routing reported complete with 0 unrouted nets. Thermal pad detection for U1/U2 is appropriate for VSON/VQFN packages.

findings.summary: 2-layer gerber set complete and aligned, board dimensions 80.87x60.55mm consistent with PCB

correct.description: All 9 expected layers present (F.Cu, B.Cu, F/B Mask, F/B Paste, F/B SilkS, Edge.Cuts) plus PTH/NPTH drills. Layer extents aligned. Dimensions from gbrjob match PCB analysis within rounding. Gerber generated by KiCad 8.0.3.
